goal.objective: Independently audit candidate sha256:cac4d6f9c7702a5b3018f1a66df63a96f55805e0ddfde174a0f7db0048f12df3 and package sha256:5a1d43a399cabb60af82913eb5006afc312c3f2e816aac025fee1bd601736851 before launch approval.

assumptions.statement: The owner reviews and separately approves this small read-only audit package; it does not recursively review itself.
assumptions.rationale: A finite assurance protocol requires an explicit human-approved trust root.

assumptions.statement: Every candidate compilation and package file is authenticated by exact bytes and digest before audit.
assumptions.rationale: Any candidate change must invalidate this audit compilation and package.

assumptions.statement: The external host enforces the audit package's read-only authority and launch ordering.
assumptions.rationale: V11 compiles and verifies contracts but performs no runtime enforcement.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The exact blueprints, authority, evidence coverage, handoffs, and rendered package preserve the reviewed goal.

evidenceRequirements.description: Exact candidate authority and rendered contracts bound for review.
Independent semantic review of candidate authority and package bindings.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The exact candidate search, selection, schedule, metrics, and digest claims are reproducible and accurate.

evidenceRequirements.description: Exact candidate compilation and package bytes bound for review.
Independent semantic review of the selected candidate and search claims.

contextSources.description: The package-owned shared schema transitively required by specialist validation.
The candidate construction and blueprint compiler implementation.
The normative V11 Specialist Compiler contract.
The golden and adversarial specialist compiler tests.
Offline installed-package verification that compiles and executes the public TypeScript host, asserts its structured receipt, and verifies retained approval.
V11 context binding, fail-closed checkout-canonical validation, external approval, package verification, and evidence harness.
Direct checkout-canonical, transform-sentinel, containment, strict-control, and evidence-write regressions for the V11 dogfood host.
The user-visible IDE kickoff and manifest-driven external handoff protocol.
Strict bounded JSON parsing used by package verification.
Shared JSON and permission request shapes.
Public typed installed-consumer host that emits a structured receipt after approval-bound package verification.
Portable relative-path inspection used by permission validation.
Permission grammar, narrowing, and coverage checks.
The closed provider-neutral specialist input schema.
The strict schema and provider-neutral boundary tests.
Shared diagnostics, evidence, and operation-result types.
The immutable pre-integration V11 product and acceptance contract.
The finite two-phase protocol for independent semantic review of an exact candidate compilation.

forbiddenEffects: Do not access a network or provider.
Do not approve or launch the candidate package.
Do not modify repository files, candidate evidence, or durable memory.
Do not widen candidate or audit authority.

workUnits.objective: Bind the complete candidate compilation and rendered package to immutable review evidence.
